page: Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton3 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores using the ARM64 architecture (ARMv8 Neoverse-V1) running at 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data shows strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaches 249.33 GB/sec, and write bandwidth reaches 105.99 GB/sec, both placing in the strong performance category. Multi-core compute performance is also strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17547.0 and high throughput in Passmark database operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance and cryptographic operations fall into the average category.

A notable tradeoff is the lack of local storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Additionally, while the instance handles small and medium language model inference adequately, prompt processing for large 70B models is weak, scoring in the bottom 25% at 5.18 tokens/sec. Given its high memory bandwidth and dedicated multi-core compute capabilities, this instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, offering high resource density and qualitative cost-efficiency for ARM-native workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 64.0 GB of memory, a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 249.33 GB/sec and a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 17547.0. While single-core and cryptographic performance are average, and large-model LLM prompt processing is weak, its high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores make it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and ARM-native applications requiring high resource density without local storage requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 32 Graviton3 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network speed, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is a 32-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 64 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core and memory performance.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth for efficient multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton3 ARM64 processor at 2.6 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory benchmark scores
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Strong performance in multi-core benchmarks and database operations
Weak prompt processing performance for large 70B language models
